title: Moranopteris gradata
taxonomy: Moranopteris gradata is classified in the family Polypodiaceae, commonly known as the spleenwort family, and is a species of fern. It belongs to the genus Moranopteris which is a group of vittarioid ferns. Moranopteris gradata is native to tropical and subtropical regions of Central and South America. The classification of this species is further supported by its morphological characteristics, such as its pinnae and frond structure. Overall, the classification of Moranopteris gradata reflects its evolutionary relationships with other fern species within the family Polypodiaceae.

The plant is used in the treatment of various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal problems. Its leaves and stems are said to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can aid in wound healing and infection prevention.
